title: Ravidas ji Chalisa
intro: Shri Ravidas Chalisa: Full Path, Meaning, and Benefits - Hymn to Guru Ravidas
description:
|| Shri Ravidas Chalisa ||

|| Doha ||
Bandau Veena Paani ko.
Dehu aay mohin gyaan.
Paay buddhi Ravidas ko.
Karau charitra bakhaan.
Maatu ki mahima amit hai.
Likhi na sakat hai daas.
Taate aayon sharan mein.
Purvahu jan ki aas.

|| Chaupai ||
Jai hovai Ravidas tumhari.
Kripa karahu harijan hitkari.
Rahu bhakt tumhare taata.
Karma naam tumhari maata.
Kashi dhing Maadur sthaana.
Varan achhoot karat gujraana.
Dwaadash varsh umra jab aayi.
Tumhare man Hari bhakti samayi.
Ramanand ke shishya kahaye.
Paay gyaan nij naam badhaye.
Shastra tark Kashi mein keenhon.
Gyaanin ko updesh hai deenhon.
Gang maatu ke bhakt apaara.
Kaudi deenh unhin upahaara.
Pandit jan taako lai jaayi.
Gang maatu ko deenh chadhayi.
Haath pasaari leenh chaugaani.
Bhakt ki mahima amit bakhani.
Chakit bhaye pandit Kashi ke.
Dekhi charit bhav bhay naashi ke.
Ral jatit kangan tab deenhon.
Ravidas adhikari keenhon.
Pandit deejau bhakt ko mere.
Aadi janam ke jo hain chere.
Pahunche pandit dhig Ravidaasa.
Dai kangan purai abhilasha.
Tab Ravidas kahi yah baata.
Doosar kangan laavahu taata.
Pandit jan tab kasam uthayi.
Doosar deenh na Ganga maayi.
Tab Ravidas ne vachan uchare.
Pandit jan sab bhaye sukhare.
Jo sarvada rahai man changa.
Tau ghar basti maatu hai Ganga.
Haath kathauti mein tab daara.
Doosar kangan ek nikaara.
Chit sankochit pandit keenhein.
Apne apne maarag leinhein.
Tab se prachalit ek prasanga.
Man changa to kathauti mein Ganga.
Ek baar phiri paryo jhamela.
Mili panditjan keenhon khela.
Saalig Raam Gang utaraavai.
Soi prabal bhakt kahalaavai.
Sab jan gaye Gang ke teera.
Moorati tairaavan bich neera.
Doob gayi sabki majhdhaara.
Sabke man bhayo dukh apaara.
Patthar moorati rahi utaraayi.
Sur nar mili jaykaar machayi.
Rahyo naam Ravidas tumhaara.
Machyo nagar mahan haahaakaara.
Cheeri deh tum dugdh bahayo.
Janam janeu aap dikhao.
Dekhi chakit bhaye sab nar naari.
Vidvaanan sudhi bisari saari.
Gyaan tark Kabira sang keenhon.
Chakit unahun ka tum kari deenhon.
Guru Gorakhahi deenh updesha.
Un maanyo taki sant vishesha.
Sadana peer tark bahu keenhon.
Tum taako updesh hai deenhon.
Man mahan haaryo Sadan kasai.
Jo Dilli mein khabari sunayi.
Muslim dharm ki suni kubadaayi.
Lodhi Sikandar gayo gussaayi.
Apne grih tab tumhin bulaava.
Muslim hon hetu samujhaava.
Maani nahin tum uski baani.
Bandigrih kaati hai raani.
Krishn darash paaye Ravidaasa.
Safal bhayi tumhari sab aasha.
Taale tuti khulyo hai kaara.
Maam Sikandar ke tum maara.
Kashi pur tum kahan pahunchaayi.
Dai prabuta arumaan badayi.
Meera Yogavati guru keenhon.
Jinko Kshatriya vansh praveeno.
Tinko dai updesh apaara.
Keenhon bhav se tum nistaara.

|| Doha ||
Aise hi Ravidas ne.
Keenhein charit apaar.
Koi kavi gaavai kitai.
Tahoon na paavai paar.
Niyam sahit harijan agar.
Dhyan dharai Chalisa.
Taaki raksha karenge.
Jagatpati Jagdeesha.
meaning:
|| Shri Ravidas Chalisa ||

|| Doha ||
Bandau Veena Paani ko.
I bow to Goddess Saraswati (the one with the Veena).
Dehu aay mohin gyaan.
Please grant me knowledge.
Paay buddhi Ravidas ko.
Finding the wisdom of Ravidas.
Karau charitra bakhaan.
I narrate his character.
Maatu ki mahima amit hai.
The Mother's glory is infinite.
Likhi na sakat hai daas.
This servant cannot write it all.
Taate aayon sharan mein.
Therefore, I have come to your refuge.
Purvahu jan ki aas.
Please fulfill the wishes of your servant.

|| Chaupai ||
Jai hovai Ravidas tumhari.
Victory to you, O Ravidas.
Kripa karahu harijan hitkari.
Show mercy, O benefactor of the Harijans.
Rahu bhakt tumhare taata.
Rahu was your father (a devotee).
Karma naam tumhari maata.
Your mother's name is Karma.
Kashi dhing Maadur sthaana.
Located near Kashi in the place called Madur.
Varan achhoot karat gujraana.
Living life as an untouchable.
Dwaadash varsh umra jab aayi.
When you reached the age of twelve.
Tumhare man Hari bhakti samayi.
Devotion to Hari filled your mind.
Ramanand ke shishya kahaye.
You were called a disciple of Ramanand.
Paay gyaan nij naam badhaye.
Gaining knowledge, you enhanced your name.
Shastra tark Kashi mein keenhon.
You held a scriptural debate in Kashi.
Gyaanin ko updesh hai deenhon.
You gave teachings to the scholars/wise.
Gang maatu ke bhakt apaara.
You are an infinite devotee of Mother Ganga.
Kaudi deenh unhin upahaara.
You gave her a cowrie (shell) as an offering.
Pandit jan taako lai jaayi.
The scholars took it from there.
Gang maatu ko deenh chadhayi.
And offered it to Mother Ganga.
Haath pasaari leenh chaugaani.
She spread her hands and took the offering (the cowrie).
Bhakt ki mahima amit bakhani.
She described the infinite glory of the devotee.
Chakit bhaye pandit Kashi ke.
The scholars of Kashi were amazed.
Dekhi charit bhav bhay naashi ke.
Seeing the character of the one who destroys worldly fear.
Ral jatit kangan tab deenhon.
Then she gave a jewel-encrusted bracelet.
Ravidas adhikari keenhon.
And made Ravidas worthy of it.
Pandit deejau bhakt ko mere.
The scholar said, "Give this to my devotee."
Aadi janam ke jo hain chere.
"Who has been my servant since the beginning of birth."
Pahunche pandit dhig Ravidaasa.
The scholars reached Ravidas.
Dai kangan purai abhilasha.
And gave the bracelet, fulfilling their desire.
Tab Ravidas kahi yah baata.
Then Ravidas said this.
Doosar kangan laavahu taata.
"Brother, please bring the second bracelet."
Pandit jan tab kasam uthayi.
The scholars then took an oath.
Doosar deenh na Ganga maayi.
"Mother Ganga did not give a second one."
Tab Ravidas ne vachan uchare.
Then Ravidas spoke words.
Pandit jan sab bhaye sukhare.
Which brought happiness to the scholars.
Jo sarvada rahai man changa.
"He whose mind is always pure."
Tau ghar basti maatu hai Ganga.
"For him, Mother Ganga dwells in his home."
Haath kathauti mein tab daara.
Then he put his hand in the trough.
Doosar kangan ek nikaara.
And brought out another bracelet.
Chit sankochit pandit keenhein.
The scholars were humbled (their minds hesitated).
Apne apne maarag leinhein.
And went their separate ways.
Tab se prachalit ek prasanga.
Since then, a proverb is prevalent.
Man changa to kathauti mein Ganga.
"If the mind is pure, the Ganges is in the trough."
Ek baar phiri paryo jhamela.
Once again, a problem arose.
Mili panditjan keenhon khela.
The scholars played a game together.
Saalig Raam Gang utaraavai.
"Whosoever makes the Shaligram float in the Ganges."
Soi prabal bhakt kahalaavai.
"He alone will be called the powerful devotee."
Sab jan gaye Gang ke teera.
Everyone went to the bank of the Ganges.
Moorati tairaavan bich neera.
To make the idol float in the water.
Doob gayi sabki majhdhaara.
Everyone's idol sank in the current.
Sabke man bhayo dukh apaara.
Everyone felt immense sorrow in their hearts.
Patthar moorati rahi utaraayi.
The stone idol (of Ravidas) remained floating.
Sur nar mili jaykaar machayi.
Gods and humans together raised shouts of victory.
Rahyo naam Ravidas tumhaara.
Your name, O Ravidas, remained.
Machyo nagar mahan haahaakaara.
A clamor arose in the city.
Cheeri deh tum dugdh bahayo.
You tore your body and milk flowed out.
Janam janeu aap dikhao.
And showed your birth-sacred thread.
Dekhi chakit bhaye sab nar naari.
Seeing this, all men and women were amazed.
Vidvaanan sudhi bisari saari.
The scholars lost all their senses.
Gyaan tark Kabira sang keenhon.
You held knowledge debates with Kabir.
Chakit unahun ka tum kari deenhon.
You amazed him as well.
Guru Gorakhahi deenh updesha.
You gave teachings to Guru Gorakhnath.
Un maanyo taki sant vishesha.
He recognized you as a special saint.
Sadana peer tark bahu keenhon.
Sadana Peer debated you much.
Tum taako updesh hai deenhon.
You gave him teachings too.
Man mahan haaryo Sadan kasai.
Sadan the butcher lost his heart (was defeated).
Jo Dilli mein khabari sunayi.
News of which was heard in Delhi.
Muslim dharm ki suni kubadaayi.
Hearing the criticism of the Muslim religion.
Lodhi Sikandar gayo gussaayi.
Sikandar Lodi became angry.
Apne grih tab tumhin bulaava.
He called you to his house.
Muslim hon hetu samujhaava.
And tried to persuade you to become Muslim.
Maani nahin tum uski baani.
You did not accept his words.
Bandigrih kaati hai raani.
You endured imprisonment.
Krishn darash paaye Ravidaasa.
Ravidas received the vision of Krishna.
Safal bhayi tumhari sab aasha.
All your wishes were fulfilled.
Taale tuti khulyo hai kaara.
The locks broke and the prison opened.
Maam Sikandar ke tum maara.
You crushed the ego of Sikandar.
Kashi pur tum kahan pahunchaayi.
He had you taken back to Kashi.
Dai prabuta arumaan badayi.
And bestowed upon you lordship and honor.
Meera Yogavati guru keenhon.
Meera Yogavati accepted you as her Guru.
Jinko Kshatriya vansh praveeno.
Who was expert in the Kshatriya lineage.
Tinko dai updesh apaara.
You gave her infinite teachings.
Keenhon bhav se tum nistaara.
And liberated her from the worldly existence.

|| Doha ||
Aise hi Ravidas ne.
In this way, Ravidas.
Keenhein charit apaar.
Performed infinite acts.
Koi kavi gaavai kitai.
No matter how much a poet sings.
Tahoon na paavai paar.
He cannot find the end.
Niyam sahit harijan agar.
If a Harijan, with discipline.
Dhyan dharai Chalisa.
Meditates on this Chalisa.
Taaki raksha karenge.
He will protect them.
Jagatpati Jagdeesha.
The Lord of the Universe, Jagdeesh.

faq.q1: What is the main significance of reciting the Ravidas Chalisa?
faq.a1: Saint Ravidas is a symbol of equality, love, and devotion. Reciting this Chalisa helps destroy ego and awakens a sense of humanity, brotherhood, and true devotion towards God.
faq.q2: Does reciting it help in removing social prejudices?
faq.a2: Yes, Saint Ravidas always spread the message of ending discrimination. Reciting his Chalisa fosters a feeling of respect and equality for all beings in the heart.
faq.q3: What is the best time to recite the Ravidas Chalisa?
faq.a3: You can recite it at any time. However, reciting it on Magh Purnima (the birth anniversary of Saint Ravidas) is considered especially special and meritorious.
faq.q4: Is any specific place required to read it?
faq.a4: No, Saint Ravidas believed in the principle of "Man Changa To Kathauti Mein Ganga" (If the heart is pure, the Ganges is in the bowl). You can recite it with full devotion in a quiet corner of your home.
faq.q5: Does the Ravidas Chalisa provide mental peace?
faq.a5: Absolutely. The teachings of Saint Ravidas are simple and touch the heart. Contemplating his thoughts helps resolve inner conflicts and brings supreme peace.
